text: Operative management included cystotomy and Kapitonich approach via robotic surgery with the da Vinci X-Eye system.
An 8 mm camera port was placed at the intersection of the left 7th intercostal space and the humeral head.
A port site was opened at the intersection of the 6th intercostal space and the anterior axillary line where Cadiere forceps would be used.
The third port was an 8 mm port on the scapula tip in the seventh dorsal intercostal space.
This port was placed the Maryland dissector that was handled by the surgeon's right hand.
The assistant port was a 12 mm port on the anterior axillary line in the eighth intercostal space.
After opening the inferior pulmonary ligament,
200 cc of iodopovidon was administered into the thoracic cavity to prevent contamination due to cystic fluid spillage.
The cyst fluid was aspirated using a handmade thoracoscopic needle through the assistant port.
The cyst wall was opened with Maryland Dissector and the cystic cavity was irrigated with Iodopovidon.
The cystic membrane inside was then placed in an endobag and removed from the thorax.
Then, Capitonage was applied by robotic suturing with two zero-absorbable Vicryl sutures.
The procedure was completed without complications.
A single 24F chest drain was inserted.
